_rationale: Iter-9: C++ MCP get_context-by-id now resolves through ref_tracker.get_enhanced_symbol (O(1) hash lookup, no allocation in resolution path). Switched corpus from multi-lang to single-file: probing iter-9 showed Go's symbol_id assignment is non-deterministic on multi-file corpora (parallel scan races — Go picked d.rs / a.go / c.cpp::add for id='B' across consecutive runs against the same corpus). single-file corpus has exactly one indexable symbol (main.go::main) so id='B' is unambiguously stable across both binaries. Probe (sf corpus): GO emits {file_path,line:5,object_id:'B',symbol_type:'function',symbol_name:'main',is_exported:false,definition:'main',context:['main'],purity:{...}}. C++ emits identical non-purity fields. result.content[].text now in STABLE tier (envelope + body). Purity sub-block value-ignored via inner-canonicalize default (contexts[].purity in normalize_mcp_inner_text) — C++ emits conservative defaults (is_pure:false, purity_score:0, confidence:low) because SideEffectAnalyzer.results() is empty at MCP runtime; wiring the analyzer into the indexing pipeline is a separate follow-up task filed under the loop. Go's multi-file id-assignment non-determinism is a Go-binary concern outside the C++ port scope.

tiers._rationale_stable: Full envelope + body parity. Inner text undergoes canonicalize_json (sorted keys, ignore_paths) before comparison; purity sub-block excluded via contexts[].purity default ignore in normalize_mcp_inner_text. Resolution path: id='B' -> decode_symbol_id (base-63) -> ref_tracker.get_enhanced_symbol (absl::flat_hash_map O(1)) -> Go-shape JSON.
tiers._rationale_ignore: Standard JSON-RPC envelope noise plus timing residue. result.content[].text is now stable — see _rationale_stable for the structural delta covered by inner-canonicalize.
